statement.p: Select all rational functions. There are several correct answers.

solution.p: Definition: A rational function is a function that can be written as the ratio of two polynomials where the denominator isn't zero, as in f(x)=\frac{p(x)}{q(x)}, where p(x) and q(x) are both polynomials.
Note that a constant function like g(x)=4 is a polynomial.
A polynomial can have radical coefficient, like s(x)=\frac{\sqrt{2} x^2 + 4 x - 8}{4 - 8 x^{2}}.
But a polynomial may not have radical terms, like n(x)=\frac{2 x^2 + 4 \sqrt{x} - 8}{4 - 8 x^{2}}.
A polynomial may not have x terms inside absolute value symbols, like c(x)=\frac{2 x^2 + 4 x - 8}{4 + \lvert x \rvert }
